transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
I have a sore throat.
When did your sore throat start?
2 days ago.
Do you have any fever, chills, or night sweats?
I have a fever. I've had a fever, and I've had chills.
Do you have any difficulty swallowing or breathing?
No night sweats.
Any swollen lymph nodes or rash?
I have no difficulty swallow swallowing or breathing, and no rash.
Have you noticed any cough or nasal congestion along with your sore throat?
But I do have a lot of pain with swallowing.
Any exposure to sick contacts or recent travel?
I have no cough or runny nose.
Have you noticed any swollen glands in your neck or jaw area?
Yes.
Have you noticed any hoarseness or changes in your voice?
Yes.
Have you noticed any difficulty breathing or stridor?
No.
Have you tried any treatments or medications for your sore throat so far?
I tried NyQuil, but it didn't help.
How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
No.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Email.
Is pregnancy possible? (yes/no/unknown)
No.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you have an infection causing your sore throat, likely something like strep throat or another type of throat infection. Based on what you told me, medication treatment may be appropriate. A licensed physician will review this note, and if appropriate, may prescribe the recommended antibiotics. For now, you can take acetaminophen (Tylenol) or ibuprofen (Advil) if you need pain or fever relief and can take them safely. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or run tests. If you develop trouble breathing, severe throat pain, difficulty swallowing, or a very high fever, please seek in-person care the same day at urgent care or an emergency room. A physician will review your information and decide on the best treatment, and if needed, testing like a throat culture may be recommended.
